topic: What manual HR really costs over a full year when you total every hidden expense
format: Open with a question that forces the reader to calculate their own answer — time lost, money wasted, or risk carried
post:
How many hours did your HR team spend on payroll last month?

Not processing it. Calculating it. Re-checking it. Fixing the errors. Chasing missing attendance sheets. Reformatting the WPS file. Then doing it again after someone flagged a mistake.

Most HR managers in Oman say 2–3 days per cycle.

That's 24–36 days a year. On one task.

Now add the rest:

Leave tracking done in WhatsApp threads.
Contract renewals discovered after they've already expired.
GOSI filings cross-referenced manually between spreadsheets.
A WPS rejection that delays salaries for 200 people — on a Thursday.

None of this shows up as a line item in your budget.

But it costs you in payroll errors, late penalties, staff turnover, and the quiet stress your HR manager carries every single month.

The real number — when you add time, risk, and rework — is almost always higher than business owners expect.

If you ran HR manually this year, what do you think it actually cost you?
